rationale.R1.2: The artifact builds a logical chain: traditional estimators fail → three prior lines of work → two open problems remain → CalibCard addresses both. Checking whether that chain is sound is a natural reading of 'check this.'
rationale.R1.3: The text asserts specific numerical results (median q-error 1.7 vs 1.8, 99th-pct q-error 89→14, 91% empirical coverage at 90% nominal) with no tables or figures present; verifying that these figures are internally consistent and not overclaimed is central to any factual check.
rationale.R1.6: 'Check this' is frequently a request for proofreading or prose quality review; the writing is generally clean but sentence-level issues (e.g., run-on in opening sentence) are worth flagging if polish is the intent.
rationale.R1.7: Three specific prior works are named with author, year, benchmark result, and capability claims (e.g., 'Naru … median q-errors below 2 on JOB-light'); accuracy of those attributions is directly verifiable and could be load-bearing if the goal is a citation/scholarship check.
